note: Recorded AI responses for the bundled sample report, keyed by cluster fingerprint. Used by scripts/demo-enriched.ts to build the published demo deterministically, without an API key. Each entry is validated by the same parser that guards live model output.

5d16ee3b2eb80cbb.title: Locked-out user gets no error message on login
5d16ee3b2eb80cbb.rootCauseHypothesis: The error banner ([data-test="error-banner"]) is no longer rendered on failed login — most plausibly removed or renamed in a recent UI change, since the failure is stable across Chromium and Firefox and both affected tests wait for the same element.
5d16ee3b2eb80cbb.severityRationale: A locked-out user receives no feedback at all and cannot tell why login failed; the failure is deterministic and browser-independent.
reproSteps: Sign in with a locked-out user's credentials
Observe that the page stays on the login form with no error message shown

b741a04ad3aba6fd.title: Checkout never reaches the confirmation page after placing an order
b741a04ad3aba6fd.rootCauseHypothesis: Confirming the order does not navigate to checkout-complete.html. Because the test waits on the URL rather than a fixed delay, the redirect after order submission is likely broken, not slow — a regression in the order-confirmation step.
b741a04ad3aba6fd.severityRationale: The purchase flow cannot be completed end to end; this is the primary revenue path.
reproSteps: Add two items to the cart
Proceed to checkout and fill in the shipping information
Observe that the order-confirmation page never loads

c042707e51d2696e.title: Cart badge is intermittently empty right after adding an item (WebKit)
c042707e51d2696e.rootCauseHypothesis: A race between the add-to-cart state update and the badge render: the assertion reads the badge before the counter updates. It passed on retry and only appears on WebKit, which points to test timing rather than an application defect.
c042707e51d2696e.severityRationale: No user-facing defect confirmed; the cost is CI noise and retry time until the test waits on the state change explicitly.
reproSteps: Add one item to the cart
Read the cart badge immediately afterwards
Occasionally the badge has not been updated yet when it is read

synthesis.summary: Two real regressions and one flaky test — not a systemic outage. Failed login gives the user no feedback (stable on Chromium and Firefox), and checkout never reaches order confirmation. The cart-badge failure passed on retry and looks like test timing on WebKit.
synthesis.recommendation: Fix the checkout confirmation redirect first — it blocks the purchase flow — then restore the login error banner; give the cart-badge test an explicit wait on the cart state instead of retrying.
